summary: Stage 2 confirms Stage 1's audit. Arkansas's substantive findings on the five fields (no statutory maximum term; rebid not required for transportation services; renewal mechanics N/A; no separate special-ed contracting regime; district variance Unknown) are independently corroborated. The principal defect is unchanged and confirmed by live re-fetch: every Arkansas Code citation in source-ledger (14 rows) is paired with the IDENTICAL generic LexisNexis portal URL (https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/arcode/), which on live WebFetch issues a 301 redirect to https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=... and renders as a generic JavaScript Lexis container with no pinpoint deep-link, no readable section text, and no navigation context. The research-findings 'Sources Consulted' note candidly admits verbatim text was read via Justia (Tier-2) as a pathfinder, but the source-ledger labels every row Tier 1 and verification-notes claims 'eight load-bearing primary citations... each re-opened... all returned verified' — a defaulted/stub verdict, since re-opening the same generic portal eight times cannot pinpoint-re-verify eight different sections. Stage 2 also independently confirms that true Tier-1 pinpoint Arkansas sources exist and were not used: (a) Arkansas DESE PDF of §§ 6-19-101 through 6-19-128 at https://dese.ade.arkansas.gov/Files/20210208151716_6-19-101_through_6-19-128.pdf (search snippet confirms it contains § 6-19-102 verbatim); (b) SAS-DFA Procurement Laws and Rules July 2024 PDF at https://sas.arkansas.gov/wp-content/uploads/Procurement-Laws-and-Rules-July-2024.pdf; (c) DPSAFT PDF of § 6-21-304 at https://dpsaft.ade.arkansas.gov/Files/6-21-304_201020113357.pdf. Direct WebFetch on these official PDFs failed with TLS cert verification errors from the audit host (`unable to verify the first certificate`) and Firecrawl is blocked by MCP re-auth — so Stage 2 reaches them only through Google index and snippet evidence, mirroring Stage 1's environment limitation. Stage 2 ran disconfirming WebSearches against the $20,000 bidding-threshold claim (one snippet quoted '$10,000', initially suggesting a possible substantive defect) and resolved that the $10,000 figure refers to an earlier/superseded version; the current statutory base is $20,000 with CPI adjustment, eff. Act 2021 No. 262 — Stage 1's number is substantively correct. Disconfirming sweep also confirmed no 2025 Act amends the load-bearing sections and no separate Arkansas SPED procurement regime exists. Citation hygiene and source-tier discipline defects from Stage 1 hold as P1 / P2. Stage 2 keeps the overall verdict Material and reconciles the issues list with two minor adjustments: (i) Issue AR-C1-02 (DPSAFT URLs) re-confirmed as transient cert issue not unreachable — Stage 2 finds Google index continues to surface both URLs and substantive content (6 CAR pts. 334-337) corroborated via codeofarrules.arkansas.gov; (ii) Issue AR-C2-01 (§ 19-11-238 stylistic) and AR-C5-01 (POSITIVE finding, completeness) preserved as-is. No P1 substantive issue found by Stage 2; no Stage 1 defect dissolved.
loadBearingNotReVerifiedList: Ark. Code Ann. § 6-19-102 (load-bearing for Max Term cell) — paired with generic portal URL https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/arcode/; live WebFetch confirms 301 redirect to a generic Lexis container with no pinpoint; section text never re-opened on an official pinpoint source during Stage-2 SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT. Substance corroborated externally via WebSearch (DESE official PDF returns § 6-19-102 verbatim) but the source-ledger URL does not pinpoint.

Ark. Code Ann. § 6-13-620 (load-bearing for Max Term + general board contracting authority) — same generic portal URL; not pinpoint-re-opened. Substance corroborated only by reference; the DESE PDF for §§ 6-19-101–6-19-128 does not contain Ch. 13 sections; no equivalent Tier-1 pinpoint URL was located by either stage.
Ark. Code Ann. § 6-21-301 (load-bearing for Rebid scope — 'personal and professional services' excluded from commodities) — same generic portal URL; not pinpoint-re-opened on an official source.

Ark. Code Ann. § 6-21-304 (load-bearing for Rebid — $20,000 commodities-only threshold) — same generic portal URL; not pinpoint-re-opened. Live WebSearch independently confirms the $20,000 figure (Act 2021 No. 262); DPSAFT pinpoint PDF (https://dpsaft.ade.arkansas.gov/Files/6-21-304_201020113357.pdf) exists per Google index and remains UNUSED by the ledger. Stage 2 live WebFetch on the DPSAFT PDF returned TLS cert verification error from audit host (same limitation as Stage 1).
Ark. Code Ann. § 19-11-203 (load-bearing for applicability — political subdivisions vs. state agencies definitions) — same generic portal URL; not pinpoint-re-opened on an official source.

Ark. Code Ann. § 19-11-238 (load-bearing for inapplicability cross-reference — 7-year cap not reaching school districts) — same generic portal URL; not pinpoint-re-opened. SAS-DFA Procurement Laws and Rules July 2024 PDF (https://sas.arkansas.gov/wp-content/uploads/Procurement-Laws-and-Rules-July-2024.pdf) exists per Google index and remains UNUSED by the ledger; Stage 2 live WebFetch on this PDF also returned TLS cert verification error.

citationVerifications.claimedTierOrUrl: Tier 1 — official Arkansas Code (state-sanctioned LexisNexis portal); URL https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/arcode/
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Stage 2 live WebFetch (2026-06-24) on https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/arcode/ returned a 301 redirect to https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=00JAA3ZTU0NTIzYy0zZDEyLTRhYmQtYmRmMS1iMWIxNDgxYWMxZTQKAFBvZENhdGFsb2cubRW4ifTiwi5vLw6cI1uX. The redirected page renders as a generic JavaScript Lexis container with no pinpoint deep-link, no readable section text, no navigation context — confirmed by Stage 2 follow-up WebFetch on the advance.lexis.com URL, which returned 'tracking pixel + redirect link only ... no visible text, code sections, or substantive page content.' None of the 14 cited sections is reachable from this URL without interactive in-browser navigation. This is a generic-portal-not-pinpoint defect — confirms Stage 1 AR-C1-01. Substance independently corroborated through WebSearch: (a) Arkansas DESE official PDF of §§ 6-19-101 through 6-19-128 at https://dese.ade.arkansas.gov/Files/20210208151716_6-19-101_through_6-19-128.pdf surfaces in Google with the § 6-19-102 text snippet matching the source-ledger's relevant text quote ('board of directors of each school district ... authorized to purchase vehicles ... may hire or purchase such school buses or other vehicles and hire persons to operate them, or make such other arrangements as it may deem best'); (b) Act 2021 No. 262 amendment of § 6-21-304 confirms $20,000 statutory base (CPI-adjusted), eliminating a possible $10,000 substantive concern raised by an initial Stage 2 disconfirming snippet; (c) SAS-DFA Procurement Laws and Rules July 2024 PDF independently surfaces in Google index and per Stage 1 audit research confirms § 19-11-238's '4 years original term / 7 years total' cap and § 19-11-203 definitions. Net: portal does not pinpoint; substance is verifiable on independent official sources that the ledger did not use. Per audit guardrail ('A generic portal/landing URL that does NOT reach the cited pinpoint section is partially verified at best'), final live verdict = partially verified.

Stage 2 live WebFetch returned 'timeout of 60000ms exceeded' on the lowercase-t path and 'unable to verify the first certificate' on the capital-T path (https://dpsaft.ade.arkansas.gov/Transportation/...). Firecrawl scrape was blocked by MCP re-auth (token expired) — Stage 2 explicitly surfaces this rather than retrying around it. Google index independently confirms the URL exists as the DPSAFT Rules/Laws — Transportation index page (Google index uses capital 'T' in /Transportation/ — minor casing drift from the deliverables, which use lowercase 't'). 6 CAR pts. 334, 335, 336, 337 independently confirmed via codeofarrules.arkansas.gov (Stage 1 found Part 335 live; Stage 2 WebSearch confirms Part 334 governs school-bus driver physicals/operations and other transportation rules cover bus specifications and safety — none addresses district-to-private-contractor contract term/rebid/renewal). Substantive claim plausible against the public record but not directly re-verified on the live DPSAFT page during this audit; treated 'partially verified.' Stage 1 issue AR-C1-02 (P3, transient cert issue) holds.

Same TLS cert verification error on live WebFetch. Google index independently confirms URL exists (capital 'T' in /Transportation/). Substantive claim that 'Purchasing' headings point only to bus-purchase statutes not directly re-verified on live page; substance plausible from independent Google index returns surfacing § 6-19-114 (bus purchases) and § 6-21-304 (commodity purchases) under DPSAFT-indexed content.

citationVerifications.citation: Arkansas DESE, Office of Special Education, IDEA Part B Fiscal Accountability Procedures Manual (10/31/2022)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Not separately re-opened in this audit. WebSearch independently confirms (i) Arkansas treats special-education transportation as an IDEA Part B related service and (ii) no separate state contracting regime for SPED transportation contracts is located. The substantive finding is supported, but the specific allowable-cost quote in the source-ledger was not re-confirmed on the live PDF during this audit.

citationVerifications.citation: Arkansas House of Representatives, '2025 Transportation Legislation'
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Stage 2 live WebFetch successful. Page covers Acts 16 (Blue Envelope Program), 17 (driver's license fee), 117 (work-zone safety training), 209 (construction vehicle lighting), 322 (cargo theft penalties), 327 (mobile work zone enforcement), 528 (rail infrastructure tampering), 604 (CDL English/work-authorization). None of these 2025 Acts amends the Arkansas Code provisions governing district-to-private-contractor transportation contracts (term, rebid, renewal, procurement). Stage 2 independent WebSearch on 2025 Acts amending §§ 6-13-620, 6-19-102, 6-21-304, or 19-11-238 also returned no on-point amendments. Currency check holds.

citationVerifications.citation: Arkansas School Boards Association 'Model Policies' index page
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Properly disclosed as unreachable in the deliverables. No claim attached to it; absence of documentation preserved correctly.

citationVerifications.citation: Little Rock School District procurement manual PDF
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Properly disclosed as unreachable (binary not parsed by WebFetch). Not used to establish state law.

citationVerifications.claimedTierOrUrl: Tier 3 pathfinder — acknowledged 'Stage 1 relay; not independently re-fetched'
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Acknowledged in scratch/arkansas.json sourcesUnreachable as Stage-1 relay not independently re-fetched. WebSearch independently confirms AG Op. 98-207 concerns parochial-school transport permissibility (off-point for term/rebid/renewal). Properly flagged as relay-only in deliverables; no load-bearing claim depends on it.

mismatches: scratch/arkansas.json 'sourceValidationVerdict' = 'verified' and verification-notes claim 'eight load-bearing primary citations... each re-opened one final time and all returned verified.' But the ledger gives all 14 Arkansas Code citations the SAME generic portal URL, which Stage 2 live WebFetch confirms cannot re-open any pinpoint section — defaulted/stub 'verified' verdict for the Arkansas Code rows.

research-findings 'Sources Consulted' note candidly admits 'for Arkansas and Georgia... verbatim text was read via a secondary republication used only as a pathfinder' (Justia, Tier-2). This is internally inconsistent with the source-ledger labeling every Ark. Code row as Tier 1 with the same generic Lexis portal URL — the actual pathfinder source for the text is not surfaced in the ledger.

scratch/arkansas.json adversarialOverallVerdict says 'confirmed (5/5 fields)' while challenge-matrix actually carries 7 Arkansas rows (5 required topics + currentness + school-vs-general-procurement) — verification-notes correctly says 'seven challenges'; the JSON's 5/5 framing is consistent with required-field verdicts but understates total challenge count. Cosmetic.

issues.field: Statutory Citation (Field 4) — generic portal URL fails pinpoint resolvability for the entire load-bearing Arkansas Code block
issues.evidence: Stage 2 live re-confirmed Stage 1 finding. Every Arkansas Code citation in the source-ledger (§§ 6-19-101, 6-19-102, 6-19-114, 6-13-620, 6-21-301, 6-21-303, 6-21-304, 6-21-305, 6-21-306, 19-11-202, 19-11-203, 19-11-206, 19-11-238, 19-11-249) is paired with the IDENTICAL generic portal URL https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/arcode/. Stage 2 live WebFetch: 301 redirect to https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=00JAA3ZTU0NTIzYy0zZDEyLTRhYmQtYmRmMS1iMWIxNDgxYWMxZTQKAFBvZENhdGFsb2cubRW4ifTiwi5vLw6cI1uX, which on follow-up WebFetch returned generic Lexis container content only — no pinpoint deep-link, no readable section text, no navigation context. None of the 14 cited sections can be reached from this URL without interactive in-browser navigation. The research-findings 'Sources Consulted' note discloses that verbatim text 'was read via a secondary republication used only as a pathfinder' (Justia, Tier-2). The verification-notes' claim that 'eight load-bearing primary citations... each re-opened... all returned verified' is therefore a stub/defaulted verdict — the same generic portal cannot have re-verified eight different pinpoint sections. Substance independently corroborated via WebSearch against arkleg/DESE/SAS-DFA official Arkansas state sources during Stage 2, but the source-ledger URLs themselves do not satisfy C1 pinpoint resolvability.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace the generic Lexis portal URL on each load-bearing Arkansas Code row with a true Tier-1 pinpoint official URL: (a) §§ 6-19-101 through 6-19-128 — use https://dese.ade.arkansas.gov/Files/20210208151716_6-19-101_through_6-19-128.pdf (Arkansas DESE official statutory PDF; existence confirmed by Stage 2 Google index returning the section text); (b) § 6-21-304 — use https://dpsaft.ade.arkansas.gov/Files/6-21-304_201020113357.pdf (DPSAFT official PDF; existence confirmed by Stage 2 Google index); (c) §§ 19-11-202, 203, 206, 238, 249 — use https://sas.arkansas.gov/wp-content/uploads/Procurement-Laws-and-Rules-July-2024.pdf (SAS-DFA official Procurement Laws and Rules July 2024 PDF; existence confirmed by Stage 2 Google index) with pinpoint section page noted; (d) § 6-13-620 and §§ 6-21-301, 303, 305, 306 — locate the corresponding DESE/DPSAFT/arkleg PDF or supplement with the arkleg.state.ar.us Arkansas Code Search portal at https://portal.arkansas.gov/service/arkansas-code-search-laws-and-statutes/. If a true pinpoint URL is genuinely unavailable, label the Lexis portal as 'partial — portal only; section text confirmed via Justia (Tier-2) pathfinder' rather than 'Tier 1.'

issues.evidence: Although the cited Lexis portal IS the state-sanctioned official publisher of the Arkansas Code, the URL pinpoints nothing (Stage 2 live re-confirmed), and the 'Sources Consulted' note candidly admits the verbatim section text was read via Justia (Tier-2). The source-ledger nonetheless labels every Arkansas Code row 'Tier 1' without flagging that the actual reading source for the text was Tier-2. Per the orchestrator's source-hierarchy rule, secondary summaries may be used as pathfinders but the LEDGER should disclose that role.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Either (a) move to true Tier-1 pinpoint URLs as in AR-C1-01 (preferred), or (b) split each row's URL column into 'Official Source URL (portal)' = current Lexis URL AND 'Reading URL (pathfinder)' = the actual Justia URL used to read the text, retaining the Tier-1 label only if the pinpoint section text is independently confirmed on a true Tier-1 source. Also update the verification-notes 'SOURCE-VALIDATION REVISIT' line so it no longer claims 'each re-opened one final time and all returned verified' for citations that were not pinpoint-re-opened.

issues.field: Cross-file consistency — research-findings vs source-ledger vs verification-notes vs scratch/arkansas.json
issues.evidence: scratch/arkansas.json sets sourceValidationVerdict = 'verified' and primaryCitation = 'https://www.lexisnexis.com/hottopics/arcode/'. verification-notes claims the eight load-bearing citations 'each re-opened one final time and all returned verified.' But research-findings 'Sources Consulted' note states verbatim text was actually read on a Tier-2 pathfinder (Justia), and the source-ledger reuses the same generic portal URL for all 14 Arkansas Code rows. The JSON and verification-notes overstate the source-validation revisit relative to what the research-findings note candidly discloses. Stage 2 live re-fetch on the portal URL confirms it is generic — supports this inconsistency.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Reconcile the four files. Either lower the sourceValidationVerdict for the Arkansas Code block to 'partially verified — portal-only URL; text read on Tier-2 pathfinder' across scratch/arkansas.json AND verification-notes, or upgrade the ledger URLs to true Tier-1 pinpoint sources as in AR-C1-01.

issues.field: Cross-file consistency — adversarial challenge count framing
issues.evidence: scratch/arkansas.json adversarialOverallVerdict = 'confirmed (5/5 fields)'. The challenge-matrix actually carries 7 Arkansas rows (5 required topics + currentness + school-vs-general-procurement). verification-notes correctly says 'seven challenges.' The JSON's '5/5' framing is consistent with the five required-field verdicts but understates the total challenge count.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Reframe the JSON adversarialOverallVerdict to 'confirmed (5/5 required fields; 7/7 challenge rows ran)' or note in verification-notes that '5/5' refers to required-field verdicts not total challenge rows. Cosmetic only.

issues.field: DPSAFT Rules — Transportation and Laws — Transportation URLs
issues.evidence: Both DPSAFT URLs returned 'unable to verify the first certificate' on Stage 2 live WebFetch (capital-T variant); the lowercase-t variant timed out at 60000 ms. Firecrawl was unavailable (MCP re-auth required) — Stage 2 surfaces this rather than retrying around it. Google index independently confirms the URLs exist (with capital 'T' in /Transportation/); 6 CAR pts. 334, 335 independently confirmed via codeofarrules.arkansas.gov; Stage 2 search also independently confirms substantive rule scope (school-bus driver physicals, bus specifications, maintenance/operation — no procurement command). Treated as partially verified for this audit, not unreachable. Stage 2 confirms this is a transient TLS/cert issue rather than true unreachability; URL casing in Google index differs from the deliverables (capital 'T' vs lowercase).
issues.recommendedRemediation: On the next re-verification cycle, retry these DPSAFT URLs from a host with the relevant intermediate cert chain; if persistent, normalize URL casing to whatever resolves (likely capital 'T' per Google index) and note the cert state in verification-notes.

issues.evidence: verification-notes states 'Justia 2024 Arkansas Code banner confirmed on every cited section.' By the 2026-06-23 baseline, the 2025 General Assembly Regular Session has concluded (95th GA convened 1/13/2025, adjourned sine die 5/5/2025 per Stage 2 independent search); the deliverables review the 2025 transportation Acts (16, 17, 117, 209, 322, 327, 528, 604) and confirm none amend the relevant sections — re-verified live by Stage 2 on https://www.arkansashouse.org/news/post/29560/2025-transportation-legislation. Stage 2 independent search for 2025 Acts amending §§ 6-13-620, 6-19-102, 6-21-304, or 19-11-238 also returned no on-point amendments. The '2024 Arkansas Code' banner reading is now a year stale relative to baseline but is mitigated by the 2025 session-law summary check.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add an explicit '2025 session-laws cross-checked through 2025 Regular Session end; 2026 session bills tracked' line to verification-notes, or upgrade to a 2025 Arkansas Code edition if/when Justia/Lexis publishes one.

issues.field: Adversarial robustness — district variance challenge somewhat pro-forma
issues.evidence: The district-variance challenge row reaches AG Op 2009-213 (county contracts under § 14-22-106 — off-point) and AG Op 98-207 (parochial-school transport — off-point), plus an attempted Little Rock manual fetch and an attempted ASBA fetch. A stronger disconfirming search would have sampled at least one additional large-district policy (Pulaski County Special, Bentonville, Springdale, or Fort Smith) and Arkansas Legislative Audit reports for districts. Stage 2 WebSearch during this audit independently found no documented divergent interpretations, so the 'Unknown' verdict still holds.
issues.recommendedRemediation: On the next revision, broaden the district-variance challenge to include at least two large-district transportation procurement policies (Pulaski County Special, Bentonville, Springdale, or Fort Smith) and one Arkansas Legislative Audit report touching district contracting. Even if the result remains 'Unknown,' the negative search path will be substantially stronger.

issues.evidence: Source-ledger row for § 19-11-238 quotes '(a)...not more than seven (7) years...' and '(c)(1) Original terms...shall not exceed four (4) years.' Stage 1 audit reported independent WebFetch on the SAS-DFA Procurement Laws and Rules July 2024 PDF corroborated the operative cap: 'The original term of a multiyear contract shall not exceed four (4) years, and the total term, including all renewals and extensions, shall not exceed seven (7) years.' Stage 2 live WebFetch on this SAS-DFA PDF returned 'unable to verify the first certificate' from audit host; substance corroborated via Stage 2 WebSearch returning the same operative figures (4-year initial term / 7-year total). Substance verified; minor stylistic note that the ledger's quoting uses ellipses rather than the cleaner consolidated phrasing in the official PDF.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Cosmetic — replace the ellipsed extraction with the cleaner verbatim sentence from the SAS-DFA PDF on next revision.

issues.evidence: Required coverage: at least one challenge row per state for max term, rebid, renewal, special-ed, AND district variance. Arkansas challenge-matrix carries 7 rows covering ALL five required topics plus two supplementary rows (currentness + school-specific-vs-general-procurement). Negative search paths documented for max term, rebid, renewal, and district variance. All five required-field cells populated.
issues.recommendedRemediation: No action required — C5 holds for Arkansas. Listed for completeness only.
